question.label: Opportunities wanted
question.name: What opportunities do you actually want to be explicit about?
question.description: Turns his self-advocacy thread into a concrete picture of the scope and opportunities he wants next.

answer: To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.

assessment.note: He named a concrete growth aspiration — owning a strategic bet with visible stakeholders — which is a strong positive growth signal.

new_queue.label: Direction
new_queue.name: When you imagine yourself eighteen months from now, what's actually different?

new_queue.label: Delegate more
new_queue.name: What are you still holding that someone else could own?

new_queue.label: Next experiment
new_queue.name: What's one concrete shift you want to try before our next conversation?

issues: coverage: injected wellbeing into next question (0 touches after turn 6)

history.answer_excerpt: Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
I know I want bigger scope, but I still spend too much time in execution details.
When conversations get political, I tend to retreat to what I can deliver directly.
I can run projects, but shaping cross-org alignment is where I hesitate.
Part of it is confidence, I do not want to look unprepared in senior forums.
I also have not been explicit enough about what opportunities I want.
To grow, I need to own a strategic bet with visible stakeholders.
